mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the 20-day forward return following months with rising FEDFUNDS is not negative on average over a 3-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending consumer spending weakness, leading firms to mark down earnings expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over a 2-month span
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE trigger fires over any 3-year rolling period, or if the t-statistic of the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates a self-reinforcing demand expansion that exceeds the additive effects of either series alone, lifting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the joint signal of accelerating PAYEMS and RSAFS does not outperform a simple linear combination of the two series in predicting 60-day returns, or if the interaction term has a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, decelerating retail sales foreshadow demand rollover and earnings compression because margins are already stretched, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter that do not occur in early-cycle phases.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if negative 60-day returns following RSAFS deceleration occur with equal or greater frequency during early-cycle expansions compared to late-cycle expansions, or if the conditional mean return is not significantly more negative in late-cycle regimes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production surprises market participants who extrapolate steady output, triggering immediate risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 5-day returns following month-over-month INDPRO declines are not negative on average over a 2-year rolling window, or if the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1300 thousand units signals a credit-sensitive sector contraction that transmits to broader demand through reduced construction employment and material orders, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day S&P 500 returns are non-negative in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 1300 over a 3-year rolling window, or if the mean conditional return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% restricts corporate borrowing and consumer credit, raising the probability of a hard landing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices recession risk.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 5-day returns conditional on FEDFUNDS > 5.0% are not negative on average over a 2-year rolling window, or if the trigger fails to produce a negative return in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and housing starts signals a broadening supply-demand slump that amplifies recession risk beyond either indicator alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the joint negative signal of INDPRO and HOUST does not produce more negative 20-day returns than either series alone over a 3-year rolling window, or if the interaction term carries a positive beta (p > 0.05).
